segments.text: So all the children of Israel came out, from Dan to Beersheba, as well as from the land of Gilead, and the congregation gathered together as one man before the Lord at Mizpah.
And the leaders of all the people, all the tribes of Israel, presented themselves in the assembly of the people of God, four hundred thousand foot soldiers who drew the sword.
Now the children of Benjamin heard that the children of Israel had gone up to Mizpah.
Then the children of Israel said,
Tell us, how did this wicked deed happen?
segments.speaker: Children of Israel

segments.text: So the Levite, the husband of the woman who was murdered, answered and said,
My concubine and I went into Gibeah, which belongs to Benjamin, to spend the night.
segments.speaker: Levite

segments.text: And the men of Gibeah rose against me, and surrounded the house at night because of me. They intended to kill me, but instead they ravished my concubine so that she died.
segments.speaker: Levite

segments.text: So I took hold of my concubine, cut her in pieces, and sent her throughout all the territory of the inheritance of Israel, because they committed lewdness and outrage in Israel.
segments.speaker: Levite

segments.text: Look! All of you are children of Israel; give your advice and counsel here and now!
segments.speaker: Levite

segments.text: So all the people arose as one man, saying,
None of us will go to his tent, nor will any turn back to his house;
segments.speaker: People of Israel

segments.text: but now this is the thing which we will do to Gibeah: We will go up against it by lot.
segments.speaker: People of Israel

segments.text: We will take ten men out of every hundred throughout all the tribes of Israel, a hundred out of every thousand, and a thousand out of every ten thousand, to make provisions for the people, that when they come to Gibeah in Benjamin, they may repay all the vileness that they have done in Israel.
segments.speaker: People of Israel

segments.text: Then the children of Israel arose and went up to the house of God to inquire of God.
Which of us shall go up first to battle against the children of Benjamin?
segments.speaker: Children of Israel

segments.text: Judah first!
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Then the children of Israel went up and wept before the Lord until evening, and asked counsel of the Lord, saying,
Shall I again draw near for battle against the children of my brother Benjamin?
segments.speaker: Children of Israel

segments.text: Go up against him.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: and Phinehas the son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron, stood before it in those days, saying,
Shall I yet again go out to battle against the children of my brother Benjamin, or shall I cease?
segments.speaker: Phinehas

segments.text: Go up, for tomorrow I will deliver them into your hand.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: They are defeated before us, as at first.
segments.speaker: Children of Benjamin

segments.text: Let us flee and draw them away from the city to the highways.
segments.speaker: Children of Israel

segments.text: The Lord defeated Benjamin before Israel. And the children of Israel destroyed that day twenty-five thousand one hundred Benjamites; all these drew the sword.
